text: Patient is a 67-year-old male presenting with a 3-day history of worsening shortness of breath and productive cough. Past medical history includes COPD and hypertension. O2 saturation 91% on room air. CXR shows bilateral infiltrates. Started on broad-spectrum antibiotics and supplemental oxygen.

72-year-old female admitted following a fall at home. No loss of consciousness. Complains of right hip pain. X-ray confirms right neck of femur fracture. Past history of osteoporosis. Referred to orthopaedics for surgical intervention. Bloods unremarkable.

58-year-old male with type 2 diabetes presenting with a 2-week history of a non-healing ulcer on the plantar surface of the right foot. Wound appears infected with surrounding erythema. HbA1c 9.2%. IV antibiotics commenced. Vascular surgery and diabetic foot team consulted.
